channel.title: software on Blog | gardlt.io
channel.description: Recent content in software on Blog | gardlt.io

item.title: Kubernetes Architecture - Control Plane Flows
item.description: Components The kubernetes control plane is where the resources are maintained, scheduled, deployed. The control plane consist of 4 core services and 1 optional. kube-apiserver kube-scheaduler kube-control-manager kube-cloud-control-manager (optional) etcd Deploying Manifests Deploying Kubernetes Manifest Step-By-Step Kubernetes Manifest kubectl apply -f file.yml 1a. Before applying the manifest the kube-api will validate the API manifest 1b. Validate KUBECONFIG certificates 1c. Validate against authentication Store Manifest in etcd to define the state of the pods.

item.title: K8s Manifest
item.description: spec and status Spec For objects that have a spec, you have to set this when you create the object, providing a description of the characteristics you want the resource to have: its desired state. Status The status describes the current state of the object, supplied and updated by the Kubernetes system and its components. The Kubernetes control plane continually and actively manages every object’s actual state to match the desired state you supplied.
